content.body: Further to the TLS crypto sheet I put together, I got the robots to do a survey of the web crypto and various crypto using web platform APIs, along with internal chrome usage.  The results are here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1_Ri-h_OfAKA9AouvPwj4SLLxX3z4ue9XpC1Ro__c24Y/edit?gid=1677021140#gid=1677021140
It does include https://wicg.github.io/webcrypto-modern-algos/ which is still work in progress.
Though talking of work in progress, one of my colleagues flagged a more substantive issue with Annex K as currently worded.
For algorithms not explicitly listed,  K.1.1.2.b gives criteria for their use.
It, however, assumes the model where standards organisations first develop cryptographic mechanisms in isolation, and then they are adopted.
In practice, browsers are part of the process of developing sate of the art cryptographic mechanisms.

For example with post quantum:
- In 2016, years before anyone but Google was thinking about this, we ran experiments with CECPQ1 and then CECPQ2. That informed us about the size impacts, etc.
- In early 2024, when Kyber existed, but before NIST finished up ML-KEM, we rolled that out, protecting user traffic earlier and clearing out compatibility issues ahead of time
- In late 2024, when ML-KEM was done and a TLS codepoint assigned, we swapped that out for ML-KEM
- It is now mid 2026. While TLS codepoint is assigned, to date, the actual RFC gluing it into TLS still is not published.
content.formatted_body:
For example with post quantum:

  • In 2016, years before anyone but Google was thinking about this, we ran experiments with CECPQ1 and then CECPQ2. That informed us about the size impacts, etc.
  • In early 2024, when Kyber existed, but before NIST finished up ML-KEM, we rolled that out, protecting user traffic earlier and clearing out compatibility issues ahead of time
  • In late 2024, when ML-KEM was done and a TLS codepoint assigned, we swapped that out for ML-KEM
  • It is now mid 2026. While TLS codepoint is assigned, to date, the actual RFC gluing it into TLS still is not published.

content.body: If the CRA was in force back then, would it have precluded that?
